summary: Add explicit Chromium and Electron host hash canary
acceptance_criteria: A real Chromium Dedicated Worker host hash canary boots the M2 fixture and compares hello, command/query, and save/load hashes against the Node runner baseline.
A real Electron renderer/Worker host hash canary boots the same M2 fixture and compares the same hashes against the Node runner baseline.
The canary is documented in test output well enough that future platform-host determinism claims can cite exact commands and hashes.
Electron security posture is not weakened: no Node integration, broad IPC, filesystem authority, or remote code execution is introduced for the canary.
This explicitly closes M1-GATE-FOLLOW-UP-001 before M2 exit or any platform-host determinism claim.
No gameplay, content, map-rendering, UI feature, or production dependency work is included beyond the host canary.
required_tests: Chromium host hash canary command with recorded hashes
Electron host hash canary command with recorded hashes
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M2-HOST-HASH-CANARY-001
notes: This is an explicit M1 Gate follow-up and must complete before M2 exit.
M2-SAVE-RUNTIME-001 must close first so this canary can make its required save/load hash comparison over explicit M2 runtime state.
If the canary requires changing Electron IPC or preload authority, stop and route to security_reviewer before implementation continues.
